level_signals: 5+ years in product management, applied AI, product operations, or hybrid builder role
Expected to own strategy and execution end to end
Reports to Head of Operations and AI — suggests senior IC not manager
Nice-to-have: 'Experience managing small teams' implies not currently required

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: 5+ years in product management, applied AI, product operations, or a hybrid builder role where you shipped AI-powered products or systems at scale.

Deep hands-on experience building with AI: you've prototyped, shipped, and iterated on AI tools, agents, or workflows, not just managed roadmaps about them. Technical fluency with modern AI coding harnesses (Cursor, Claude Code, Codex).
Working knowledge of core LLM concepts and systems (prompting, fine-tuning, embeddings, retrieval, evaluation) and the judgment to translate these into reliable, user-facing products.
Operational empathy: you can partner with sales, CX, finance, risk, and other business teams to deeply understand their challenges and deliver rapid AI-powered solutions.
Customer obsession - demonstrated ability to keep the customer (internal or external) front and center when building technical systems.
Strong communicator who can write crisp docs, run engaging internal training, and translate between deeply technical and non-technical audiences.
Fluency in data and AI evals: you know when to trust numbers and when to lean on principles.
Self-starter with high ownership, comfortable with autonomy and ambiguity. You default to building, not asking for permission.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience managing small teams of technical or operational professionals.
Experience at high-growth startups, especially in fintech, enterprise SaaS, or other domains requiring complex systems thinking.
Prior experience in internal enablement, change management, or rolling out new tools and workflows across an organization.
Familiarity with departmental workflows in operating teams (Sales, CX, Finance, Risk, HR, etc.).
Curiosity about commercializing internal AI work: customer-facing AI agents, AI spend management, or turning operational learnings into product.

explicitly_excluded: You prefer strategy briefings over hands-on delivery.
You lean toward AI research or building models rather than applied AI work.
You're not excited about building for and transforming the work of internal operators alongside external customers.
You're not interested in the foundational, sometimes unglamorous work of making AI systems reliable and scalable at the infrastructure level.

scope.surface: AI agents, tools, and workflows powering Ramp's Customer Experience (CX) teams and customer-facing agentic products
scope.business_model: B2B fintech / spend management SaaS — internal AI operations platform plus customer-facing AI CX products
primary_metrics_inferred: AI agent and tool performance (quality, accuracy, reliability)
Customer satisfaction and support resolution speed
Rate of improvement in customer-facing AI CX motion (daily/weekly)
core_responsibilities: Build and ship AI agents and platforms that make Ramp's CX motions faster, higher quality, and more reliable
Define standards of quality and accuracy for AI agents and human operator collaboration
Define the future of AI-powered operations and roles in the CX context
Own evaluation and iteration: build dashboards, eval systems, QA checks, and feedback systems
Tackle foundational AI infrastructure challenges in partnership with engineering
Tell the story externally through customer pitches, conferences, and content

green_flags: Clear and specific description of what has already been built (Ramp Glass, Ramp Inspect, Dojo, Sensei, etc.) — strong signal of real AI investment
Role is explicitly 'build and ship' not strategy-deck — suggests genuine hands-on scope
Encourages applications from candidates who don't check every box
Broad benefits package disclosed including flexible PTO, unlimited AI token usage, medical/dental/vision, parental leave
Reports to Head of Operations and AI — senior exposure and clear reporting line
Company at meaningful scale (70,000+ companies, $200B+ annualized spend) with demonstrated AI traction
red_flags: Location requirements are ambiguous — relocation support to NYC or SF is mentioned but whether remote is eligible is not stated
Role title is 'Product Manager' in the JD title but the body refers to 'AI Operations Manager' — possible inconsistency in role framing
Very broad scope (internal AI tools, customer-facing agents, external storytelling, infrastructure) may indicate under-resourced or unclear role boundaries
High buzzword density may obscure concrete day-to-day expectations

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from 5+ year floor, scope, and nice-to-have people management signal)
seniority.manages_people (inferred from 'nice-to-have' framing for team management, not required)
location.type (inferred as hybrid; relocation to NYC or SF mentioned but specific office policy not stated)
location.remote_us_eligible (inferred false based on relocation support framing toward NYC/SF)
